basicDetails.schemeName: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for assistance to Large Industries and Thrust Sector: Net SGST Reimbursement

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md:
The &quot;Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for assistance to Large Industries and Thrust Sector&quot; is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat. It aims to empower the state&#39;s dynamic large-industry sector, which significantly contributes to employment, GDP, and exports. Effective from October 5, 2022, to October 4, 2027, this initiative supports growth, investments, and the vision of &quot;Aatmanirbhar Gujarat&quot; and &quot;Aatmanirbhar Bharat.&quot;
The component &quot;Net SGST Reimbursement&quot; is designed to incentivize large industrial undertakings by reimbursing a portion of their SGST for a specific period (10 years), based on the category of their taluka and sector.
schemeContent.benefits_md:
**Category of Taluka****General****Thrust**Category 1100% of net SGST for 10 years upto 7.5% of eFCl p.a. 100% of net SGST for 10 years upto 8% of eFCl p.a. Category 290% of net SGST for 10 years upto 6.5% of eFCl p.a. 90% of net SGST for 10 years upto 7% of eFCl p.a. Category 380% of net SGST for 10 years upto 5% of eFCl p.a. 80% of net SGST for 10 years upto 5.5% of eFCl p.a.
**Note**: 1. The period of 10 years shall be from the date of commencement of commercial production. 1. List of Taluka categories[.](https://imd.gujarat.gov.in/Document/I%20Branch_2297_02_Nov_2020_98.pdf)
schemeContent.briefDescription: The "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for assistance to Large Industries and Thrust Sector"  aims to provide incentives to attract increased investments in the manufacturing sector to create more employment opportunities in Gujarat.

children.text: The "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for assistance to Large Industries and Thrust Sector" is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat. It aims to empower the state's dynamic large-industry sector, which significantly contributes to employment, GDP, and exports. Effective from October 5, 2022, to October 4, 2027, this initiative supports growth, investments, and the vision of "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat" and "Aatmanirbhar Bharat."

The component "Net SGST Reimbursement" is designed to incentivize large industrial undertakings by reimbursing a portion of their SGST for a specific period (10 years), based on the category of their taluka and sector.
Category of Taluka
General
Thrust
Category 1
100% of net SGST for 10 years  upto 7.5% of eFCl p.a.
100% of net SGST for 10 years upto 8% of eFCl p.a.
Category 2
90% of net SGST for 10 years  upto 6.5% of eFCl p.a.
90% of net SGST for 10 years upto 7% of eFCl p.a.
Category 3
80% of net SGST for 10 years  upto 5% of eFCl p.a.
80% of net SGST for 10 years upto 5.5% of eFCl p.a.
Note
The period of 10 years shall be from the date of commencement of commercial production.
List of Taluka categories
Registration Process:
Step-1:
Visit the
Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP)
portal. Click on "New Investor Registration" to access the registration form.
Step-2:
Fill in the Registration Form. Click on "Submit".
Step-3:
Verify the E-mail ID using the link sent on your registered E-mail ID.
Step-4:
After verification, you will be registered successfully.
Post-Registration Process:
Step-1:
Visit the
Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP)
portal. Click on "Login".
Step-2:
Enter the email address and password you used during registration.
Step-3:
Click the "Login" button to access your account.
Step-4:
Now fill in the required details and mandatory documents about your business to complete the application process.
Helpdesk
Contact Here

applicationProcess.process_md:
> **Registration Process:** **Step-1:** Visit the [Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP)](https://ifp.gujarat.gov.in/DIGIGOV/) portal. Click on "New Investor Registration" to access the registration form. **Step-2:** Fill in the Registration Form. Click on "Submit". **Step-3:** Verify the E-mail ID using the link sent on your registered E-mail ID. **Step-4:** After verification, you will be registered successfully.
> **Post-Registration Process:** **Step-1:** Visit the [Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP)](https://ifp.gujarat.gov.in/DIGIGOV/) portal. Click on "Login". **Step-2:** Enter the email address and password you used during registration. **Step-3:** Click the "Login" button to access your account. **Step-4:** Now fill in the required details and mandatory documents about your business to complete the application process.
> **Helpdesk****:** [Contact Here](https://ifp.gujarat.gov.in/DIGIGOV/IFP-pages/contact.jsp?pagedisp=static)

schemeDefinitions.name: Large Industrial Undertaking

children.text: A large industrial undertaking means an industrial undertaking in which the fixed capital investment made in Plant and Machinery is higher than INR 50 crores, and for which an Industrial Entrepreneurs Memorandum has been filed or any other license or permission as prescribed by the Government of India, has been obtained.
The large industrial undertaking must have obtained acknowledgment/ registration, as the case may be from DPIIT.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md:
A large industrial undertaking means an industrial undertaking in which the fixed capital investment made in Plant and Machinery is higher than INR 50 crores, and for which an Industrial Entrepreneurs Memorandum has been filed or any other license or permission as prescribed by the Government of India, has been obtained.
The large industrial undertaking must have obtained acknowledgment/ registration, as the case may be from DPIIT.

schemeDefinitions.name: Thrust Sector

children.text: Thrust sector
means the sector which is most potential sector and needs additional support to make them viable for global competitiveness.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: [Thrust sector](https://ic.gujarat.gov.in/documents/commondoc/2022/Atmanirbhar%20Gujarat%20Scheme%20for%20assistance%20to%20Large%20Industries%20and%20Thrust%20Sector.pdf) means the sector which is most potential sector and needs additional support to make them viable for global competitiveness.

schemeDefinitions.name: Gross Fixed Capital Investment (GFCI)

children.text: Gross Fixed Capital Investment means the investment made in iand, building, plant and machinery, utilities, tools and equipment, and other fixed assets required to manufacture the end product. Assets acquired and paid for during eligible investment period of the project under this scheme shall be considered for determining the gross fixed capital investment.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md:
Gross Fixed Capital Investment means the investment made in iand, building, plant and machinery, utilities, tools and equipment, and other fixed assets required to manufacture the end product. Assets acquired and paid for during eligible investment period of the project under this scheme shall be considered for determining the gross fixed capital investment. 
<br>

schemeDefinitions.name: Eligible Fixed Capital Investment (EFCI)

children.text: Eligible Fixed Capital Investment means following investment made on or after the date of issue of this GR and up to the last date of the eligible investment period as per para-3 of this G.R. Provided that, large industrial undertakings opting /applying under this scheme as per clause 16.2 or clause 16.4 of this scheme, the investment made during the operative period ofthe  previous scheme till the date of issue of this GR shall also be considered for calculating eligible FCI.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md:
Eligible Fixed Capital Investment means following investment made on or after the date of issue of this GR and up to the last date of the eligible investment period as per para-3 of this G.R. Provided that, large industrial undertakings opting /applying under this scheme as per clause 16.2 or clause 16.4 of this scheme, the investment made during the operative period ofthe  previous scheme till the date of issue of this GR shall also be considered for calculating eligible FCI.
<br>

schemeDefinitions.name: Date Of Commercial Production (DaCP)

children.text: In case of new industrial unit, the DoCP shall be the date of first sale bill of the product(s) for which the project has been set up. In case of expansion and/or diversification projects, the DoCP shall be the date of first sale bill of the product(s) being manufactured by the expansion and/or diversification project.

schemeDefinitions.definitions_md: In case of new industrial unit, the DoCP shall be the date of first sale bill of the product(s) for which the project has been set up. In case of expansion and/or diversification projects, the DoCP shall be the date of first sale bill of the product(s) being manufactured by the expansion and/or diversification project.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. A new large industrial undertaking, or an existing one that undertakes expansion and/or diversification, is eligible for this scheme.
   1. The large industrial undertaking must have obtained acknowledgment/registration, as the case may be from DPIIT. 
   1. The industry should commence commercial production during the scheme&#39;s operative period.
   1. Undertakings must have a separate GST registration for the eligible manufacturing unit.
   1. Compliance with SGST guidelines and no fraudulent reporting of sales.
   1. Only eligible goods manufactured in the premises qualify for reimbursement.

children.text: A new large industrial undertaking, or an existing one that undertakes expansion and/or diversification, is eligible for this scheme.
The large industrial undertaking must have obtained acknowledgment/registration, as the case may be from DPIIT.
The industry should commence commercial production during the scheme's operative period.
Undertakings must have a separate GST registration for the eligible manufacturing unit.
Compliance with SGST guidelines and no fraudulent reporting of sales.
Only eligible goods manufactured in the premises qualify for reimbursement.
